data.title: ABVC BioPharma gets $200K in cash from OncoX BioPharma
data.description:
• ABVC BioPharma (NASDAQ:ABVC) said it has received a $200K cash payment from OncoX BioPharma as part of its strategic licensing agreement for certain oncology-related products.
  • The payment is the first installment of $5 million in potential licensing fees from OncoX.
  • After this payment, the total accumulated cash payment ABVC has gotten from its three strategic partners for licensing various ABVC products is $546K.

data.title: Macy's slides after updating full-year guidance, concluding accounting investigation
data.description:
Macy's (NYSE:M) fell in early trading on Wednesday after the department store operator issued financial results and a new outlook to reflect revisions to adjust for misstated delivery expense, and the related accrual and tax effects.

As a result of an independent investigation and forensic analysis, Macy's (M) identified that a single employee with responsibility for small package delivery expense accounting intentionally made erroneous accounting accrual entries to hide approximately $151 million of cumulative delivery expenses from the fourth quarter of 2021 through the third quarter of 2024.

For Q3, Macy's (M) net sales were down 3.1%, with comparable sales down 3.0% on an owned basis and down 2.2% on an owned-plus-licensed-plus-marketplace basis. Macy's (M) management noted that the fragrances, dresses and men’s and women’s active apparel categories were strong during the quarter. Macy's (M) go-forward business comparable sales were down 2.6% on an owned basis and down 1.8% on an owned-plus-licensed-plus-marketplace basis.

Looking ahead, Macy's (M) said quarter-to-date, comparable sales continue to trend ahead of third quarter levels across the portfolio. The company sees full-year sales of $22.3 billion to $22.5 billion (midpoint $22.4 billion). Full-year adjusted EPS of $2.25 to $2.50 (midpoint $2.375) is anticipated vs. $2.73 consensus and a prior view for $2.55 to $2.90.

Shares of Macy's (M) fell 8.49% in premarket trading to $15.30 vs. the 52-week trading range of $14.06 to $22.10.

data.title: Momentus files to sell 15.41M shares of Class A common stock for holders
data.description:
• Momentus (NASDAQ:MNTS) filed a prospectus related to the proposed resale of 15.41 million common shares Class A common stock by the selling stockholders.
  • This prospectus is not an offer to sell these securities.

data.title: International Consolidate Airlines rallies after Deutsche Bank turns bullish
data.description:
Deutsche Bank upgraded International Consolidated Airlines Group S.A. (OTCPK:ICAGY) to a Buy rating on Wednesday.

Analyst Jaime Rowbotham said constrained capacity on transatlantic routes to and from the UK should help IAG to further progress pricing in 2025. He said the firm's thesis is supported by early evidence from its fares tracker and underpinned by the macro outlook for the U.S., the UK and Spain.

Looking ahead, Rowbatham and his team expect fuel prices to be a tailwind, and see scope for another year of ahead-of-consensus earnings growth in 2025.

"We expect IAG to meet the taret set at the 2023 CMD for margins to be in the 12-15% range, forecasting 14.3% in 2026 (DB EBIT +8% vs cons.), up from 11.9% in 2023 and in-line with the 2018 peak. We think the journey towards a better BA has only just begun, and a key driver of the margin uplift is the £7bn transformation program which includes >1,400 initiatives. Improvements at Aer Lingus, the continued leveraging of the Spanish platforms and growing IAG Loyalty should also help."

Shares of International Consolidated Airlines Group S.A (OTCPK:ICAGY) were up 2.7% in afternoon trading in London.

The Seeking Alpha Quant Rating on ICAGY is flashing Strong Buy.

data.title: REV Group Non-GAAP EPS of $0.51 beats by $0.02, revenue of $597.9M misses by $5.43M
data.description:
• REV Group press release (NYSE:REVG): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.51 beats by $0.02.
  • Revenue of $597.9M (-13.8% Y/Y) misses by $5.43M.
  • Net sales in the range of $2.3 to $2.4 billion vs $2.38B consensus, net income in the range of $98.0 to $125.0 million, Adjusted EBITDA in the range of $190.0 to $220.0 million, Adjusted Net Income in the range of $116.0 to $140.0 million and Free Cash Flow in the range of $90.0 to $110.0

data.title: Lowe's updates on strategy, guidance, and new initiatives ahead of key investor event
data.description:
Lowe's Companies (NYSE:LOW) released a strategy and guidance update ahead of its 2024 Analyst and Investor Conference on Wednesday. The home improvement retailer also said it plans to provide a detailed update on its key growth and productivity initiatives at the event.

"As we look ahead to the expected recovery in home improvement, we are making investments to position the company for long-term growth," stated CEO Marvin Ellison. "We are evolving our Total Home strategy to help solve our customers' total home improvement needs with more value and exceptional service," he added.

New initiatives unveiled include an AI framework to enhance customer experience and the first product marketplace in the U.S. home improvement industry

Ellison also pointed out that Lowe's (LOW) is building on its momentum with Pros now that the company reached 30% Pro penetration.

In terms of store growth, Lowe's (LOW) plans to expand its footprint and attract new DIY and Pro customers to the brand by opening 10 to 15 stores per year over the next several years in fast-growing markets in the U.S.

Lowe's (LOW) affirmed its outlook for full year 2024 guidance for total sales of $83.0 to $83.5 billion, comparable sales expected to be down -3.0% to -3.5%, adjusted operating income as a percentage of sales of 12.3% to 12.4%, and adjusted diluted earnings per share of approximately $11.80 to $11.90 vs. $11.90 consensus.

Lowe's (LOW) also sees capital expenditures of approximately $2 billion for the full year.

Shares of Lowe's (LOW) are up 31% on a year-to-date basis.

data.title: Photronics Non-GAAP EPS of $0.59, revenue of $222.63M
data.description:
• Photronics press release (NASDAQ:PLAB): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.59.
  • Revenue of $222.63M (-2.1% Y/Y).
  • IC revenue was $163.7 million, down 1% year-over-year and up 5% sequentially.
  • FPD revenue was $58.9 million, down 7% from the same quarter last year and up 7% sequentially.
  • For the first quarter of fiscal 2025, Photronics expects Revenue to be between $208 million and $216 million and non-GAAP Net income attributable to Photronics, Inc. shareholders to be between $0.43 and $0.49 per diluted share.

data.title: Google requests FTC to break up Microsoft-OpenAI's cloud deal - report
data.description:
Alphabet's (GOOG) (GOOGL) unit Google has requested the U.S. Federal Trade Commission to break up Microsoft's (NASDAQ:MSFT) exclusive deal to host OpenAI's technology on its cloud servers, The Information reported.

The discussions occurred after the U.S. regulator asked Google about Microsoft's business practices as part of a broader probe, the report added, citing a person involved in the talks.

Microsoft's competitors in the cloud server space, such as Google, also want to host OpenAI's large language models, or LLMs, so that their cloud customers do not need to access Microsoft's servers to get access to the ChatGPT-maker's technology, the report noted.

Companies that buy OpenAI's technology via Microsoft may have to see additional charges if they do not already use Microsoft's servers to carry out their operations, the report added.

Google and other rivals have highlighted that these new costs will impact customers, the report noted.

Microsoft, Google, OpenAI and the FTC did not respond to an email from Seeking Alpha till the time of publication of this report.
